reviews.review_description: I was around the volcano location when my objective was to locate the mining settlement, but when I try going there, there is a invisible wall blocking my progress.

reviews.review_title: Great, but hoping for no vignette option
reviews.review_description: Enjoying it and really like the atmosphere, but if the developers are reading this, I really hope you can add an option for no vignette. I'm moving slower than I should just to try to avoid triggering the vignette because I find them annoying and always turn them off in any game. It is great to have them as an option for people who need them, but please don't force them on people who do not want them.

developer_response.body:
UPDATE: A patch that includes the option to toggle vignette on/off is now live (1.0.680) - please update to the latest version and let us know via support@beyondbluegame.com if you encounter any issues.
--
Appreciate the feedback! We hear you and are planning to add this functionality in the next update.

reviews.review_title: Too short, not worth the money.
reviews.review_description: It saddens me to wright this I had high hopes. This game had potential but it changes mid game from exploring the reef and fostering a sence of wonder to an game of industrial sabotage, and just when you think you will get back to the exploration, nope, the game just ends. It took less than two hours to complete. It tries to be both an eco warrior and educational and fails miserably at both.

reviews.review_title: It's not great, but it has potential
reviews.review_description:
I completed the game yesterday.

The first 15 minutes are great you see a handful of sea creatures and they look majestic but the effect quickly wares off. Once you have had a chance to observe one or two creatures you quickly realise the limitations of the Quest 3 graphics and the sea creatures don't look as majestic anymore.

The game is literally about 2 hours long and consists of you swapping between a human and a robot to fix random machines by moving the parts into place and removing debris. It is split out into two parts the first hour where you explore the ocean and the second where you go into the deep dark ocean.

It sort of seems like a tech demo because of how short it is. With that being said it could be quite a good game if they make another one and massively expand the game with more creatures and perhaps a bit more action like subnautica.

But as it stands now the game isn't subnautica and isn't worth the time and money to play it in my opinion.

Just finished the game and though it is way too short it is very realistic. I am a PADI dive master in the Red Sea and this game, especially the opening areas are  incredibly similar to what I experience every day. 

 The wrist jets that the main character uses to swim quickly are also real and so much fun in this game and real life. Although unfortunately not all of the technology in the game is available in real life right now. I am looking forward to the day I can dive with a wisecracking robot who likes fist bumps.

This is a beautiful game for people who love the ocean, and for people who either can't afford to experience it in real life or are too nervous to do so.

I just hop in this game for about a hour now and I am truly blown away. The swimming control are fun a very easy to master and the ocean life is definitely a good feel and look. Lots of side missions and ocean life rescues really immerses you in this environment.
Can’t wait to explore the rest of this one . Gotta go I paused the game to write this review. So far so good 😊

developer_response.body: Wow, thanks for the kind words, Chris! Glad to hear you're loving it so far.

reviews.review_title: Good game but lasts maybe 2 hours max
reviews.review_description: I honestly felt ripped off for how little gameplay I got I wouldn’t recommend spending money on this game at least not 15$ or however much they’re charging for a 2 hour experience

I would say I'm around an hour and a half in. Thus far it is very impressive. The amount of ocean life is fantastic. The colors are vibrant and beautiful. Really brings everything to life. So far all the mechanics work well. Many different tools for the tasks at hand. Don't want to spoil it but I love the sidekick added to your journey! I just stopped to recharge my battery but plan on jumping right back in. Im at a section very deep under where things are a bit dark and Hazy. Love the changing of the environment as well. I'd strongly recommend checking this out.

developer_response.body: Appreciate you taking time to leave us a review and we are so happy to hear you are enjoying it so far!

reviews.review_description: The effects and the VR with the aquatic life is nice. However, I have encountered some issues, especially from going to the robot to the person. In stopping the first drill, after I bring down the shield, as soon I switch to the person, the electrical field is back up. Also, if I'm holding an object, I can't just put it down. Instead, it is completely gone. So, the 3 power things that I'm supposed to be putting in position after the electrical shield goes down, have now completely disappeared and I can't move on. So, I'm thinking there is a glitch or something.

developer_response.body:
Thanks again for your comments! I have some updates for you. It appears that the issues you mentioned aren't bugs but a couple tricky bits of gameplay.

Re: the Shield: Make sure that Kanaloa hits the button correctly with the puck. When placing the batteries, the static field will regenerate, so make sure to deactivate it again in order to place all the batteries!

Re: Power Cells Disappearing: Make sure you are reaching far enough back to grab the battery, it can be a bit challenging. If it disappears, try reaching for it again.

reviews.review_description: It maybe short, but it was SOOOO worth my money! I love the ocean and sea creatures since I was a kid and the game did a excellent job of making you feel like you are in the ocean!

developer_response.body: Thank you very much! We love hearing that you enjoyed it! :)

reviews.review_title: A good looking problem solving game
reviews.review_description: Graphically it looks pretty good on Quest, and the puzzles are fun and relatively easy. The only gripe I really have with it is that it's not clear when it's saving. I finish one section and move onto the next, thinking I must have reached a save point, only to find out that the next time I load, I'm restarting the last section I did. It would be good to work in a feature that says how long since you last saved.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback! I'm going to share this with the development team.

reviews.review_description: I loved exploring the ocean, swimming with the sharks, etc and getting a sense of their size. Wish there were haptics when you pet them. The game is pretty short too, maybe around 2 hours. Wish there was more. It was cool seeing Sylvia Earle was a part of it. The biggest complaint is probably the forced vignette. You will always get complaints if you don’t have the option to turn off vignette. I wasn’t sure what to do when you were supposed to shoot a puck to disable electricity. That could be more clear. I also lost progress after a save. I think it should save when a task is complete. I hope there is more to come and the game will be expanded.

reviews.review_title: My recommendation: wait for an update
reviews.review_description: I hope the developer reads this quickly and has an update that fixes the issues I mention. I'm only writing about the issues I faced in hopes they get fixed soon. If you want my full impressions you can look up my gameplay & impressions video on YouTube on my channel under FastLawyer. Anyways, the issues are: 1. slow smooth turn speed (speed set to 100 maximum) 2. automatic vignette (tunnel) when moving fast (this has been FIXED by dev) 3. clipping through the fish life and various objects 4. Difficulty activating levers (took repeated attempts) 5. visuals are kind of muddy so the 3D effect is muted (any possibility of higher resolution or higher textures? ... is this game enhanced for the Quest 3?)

Going to play this game after school and when this game was announced for the trailer and I saw there was side missions and I had to get it     Update I played it for like one hour and it it fun the story is great and love the robot

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the support!

reviews.review_description:
Obviously an early impression Review 
Saw the pre order price and the trailer and decided to dive in.
Brilliant so far, I’m learning stuff as I play.
Great colourful graphics and tons of beautiful ocean fish and mammals to scan.

So far so good, looking forward to going deeper and deeper.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review and support! Glad to hear you are digging it so far :)

reviews.review_description:
Really enjoyed this game! I think it took 2-3 hours, so you get to see a lot of different ocean scenes/"rooms" plus animals/fish/coral/rock formations — the graphics and scenery are quite beautiful, and the movement is really fluid. As a scuba diver, I loved all of this!

One thing I thought set this game apart from many other Oculus games was the quality of the tutorials and ongoing guidance throughout. The game makes it easy to see what each button does (there's a visual guide you can refer back to at any time), and the narrator helps develop your skills at a nice pace (i.e. swimming around, grabbing objects, using tools, etc.). I didn't feel overwhelmed at the beginning, as I do in other games — where it feels necessary to know what every button can/does do right from the get-go.

Other bonuses: The narrator/guide is helpful, your robot friend adds some character, and they made a good effort to create a compelling storyline. The vastness of the underwater environment is impressive; the tasks you are assigned aren't too easy but not too challenging either.

Recommendations for the developers: a) Keep the "animal info" popup dialog screens displaying a bit longer (they disappear in ~3 seconds before the user closes them, so not always easy to read them all the way through); b) introduce a way to ask for a "hint" from the narrator if you get stuck.

This was a great game thought. When I started this game I was so impressed by graphics, and the concept of making things healthy again. When getting past the amazing concept, then it turns into destroying drills. The beauty of the reef was gone. Finished this game in less than an hour. So it was not worth my time and money I spent this game. I should tell you I was excited in the beginning that I bought this for 2 other friends. Now regretting this. Hopefully the add on to this game.

reviews.review_title: Has potential, needs some fixes & extras
reviews.review_description:
Game looks great, but could use some additional fixes and features to make it a 5-star game. I will look out for updates and adjust my review accordingly. I don’t think it’s bad game, but it could be an even greater game with a few additions. Here’s a wishlist.

-Vignette toggle, as others have noted
-Haptic interactions with environment and aquatic life, as opposed to clip-through
-It would add to immersion if a swimming motion also moved you, perhaps at a lower speed than the wrist thrusters
-Encounter-type animations and dynamic interactions with sea life, e.g. react to being touched, approach or avoid AI, octopus reaching out with tentacles, curious seals, etc.
-Free explore/roam mode/map
-Sandbox/explore mode which allows you to populate the map with specific sea life, could be unlocked throughout the course of gameplay
-Journal entries with extended educational information about sea life, environment, activism, etc.

developer_response.body:
UPDATE: A patch that includes the option to toggle vignette on/off is now live (1.0.680).
--
Thank you for the feedback! I'm going to share your comments with the development team. Of note, we will be releasing an update imminently that will include a toggle for 
vignette.

reviews.review_description: Beyond Blue: After The Storm clearly emphasises its commitment to raising awareness about climate change and the conservation of marine life, aligning with the flatscreen game released in 2020. The narrative unfolds at a thoughtful pace as you investigate the damage to your research facility and uncover the causes behind the sickness affecting wildlife and plants following a tropical storm. The gameplay is intentionally slow-paced, which complements the game’s objectives without feeling overwhelming. However, there are some drawbacks, such as pop-in issues and certain control choices—most notably, the absence of an option to swim using physical motions, despite the underwater setting. Nevertheless, these minor flaws do not detract from the significant message conveyed throughout the approximately two-hour campaign and the mini-documentaries you can unlock, which are definitely worth viewing.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! I've shared your comments (and your official review) with the development team.

reviews.review_title: Needs minor work, but still Great!
reviews.review_description:
I agree with other reviews. This game looks and plays great. But it needs some tweaking. For one, not every VR gamer needs blinders. Some of us have been doing this for years, so forced blinders are really annoying. 

Also, the smooth turn speed is way too slow at its fastest setting. Even a VR vet will get nauseous with super slow smooth turning. 

Otherwise this game looks very nice and has a great locomotion system. Great voice acting and the promise of an awesome VR adventure.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words and appreciate the feedback! We're working on getting the option to disable vignette in the next patch. I'll share your note about the turn setting with the team as well.

reviews.review_description: Easy game to pickup, not demanding at all. Actually on the simplistic side, probably targeted at a younger audience. Good colorful graphics but occasionally a bit of stutter (I play on the Quest 3). Good storyline but too verbose at times. A small annoyance is that if you do a bit of exploring away from the main objective, you lose the objective arrow. It should stay on at all times, no matter how far from the objective you are. The extremely annoying thing is the presence of a vignette when you use both thrusters. No way to remove it in the settings. Otherwise I would have given it a 4 star.

developer_response.body:
UPDATE: A patch that includes the option to toggle vignette on/off is now live (1.0.680) - please update to the latest version and let us know via support@beyondbluegame.com if you encounter any issues.
--
Hi! Appreciate the review. I've shared your feedback with the team. We are planning an update which will include the ability to disable the vignette, so stay tuned!

reviews.review_description:
I’m just like anyone else I would like no vignette or at least the option to turn off. Also being able to remap some things such as propeller usage to trigger because I can’t turn when using them. Other wise this is an awesome game it’d be cool to maybe eventually have multiplayer with one player as the robot and one human! 
Update for some reason the robot has no vignette

developer_response.body:
UPDATE: A patch that includes the option to toggle vignette on/off is now live (1.0.680) - please update to the latest version and let us know via support@beyondbluegame.com if you encounter any issues.
--
Hi! Thanks so much for the review. We have heard the feedback about the vignette and are planning to include the option to disable it in the next update, so stay tuned. I'll also share your other comments with the development team.

reviews.review_description:
great atmosphere, relaxing gameplay a good price and the voice of dr. m‘benga from star trek strange new worlds:)

overall a great experience and a game for young and old altogether:)

This is seriously worth getting feels so realistic I feel like I am actually swimming under the ocean graphics are awesome who ever gives this game 1 star is straight up childish needs to grow up devs great work

developer_response.body: Thank you so much!

reviews.review_title: Great experience only possible in VR!
reviews.review_description: The game is great, any with developers support promising to remove vignette in upcoming patch I can only recommend it to every VR fan of light relaxing titles. Especially with the fair price.

developer_response.body: Thanks for your feedback! We agree and are planning to include this feature in the next patch.

reviews.review_description: At the Electrical Drill Site section of the game the power cores keep disappearing when I try to place them or if I accidentally drop them. The only way they reappear is if I quit to the main menu and come back to where I left off in the game. Basically I keep having to restart the level to make them reappear. Is this a bug? I’m stuck! Please help.

developer_response.body: Hi! Thanks for the bug report. I've shared your comments with the development team - would you mind emailing us at support@beyondbluegame.com with these details as well as your device specs in case there are any additional questions while we try to reproduce the issue?

reviews.review_description: Only played about an hour or two so still finding my flippers but soo far very enjoyable, well scripted dialogue, very good mechanics with regards tool selection and movement, cant wait to jump back in!

developer_response.body: Appreciate the review! Thank you.

reviews.review_description: Great so far but can you PLEASE add the option of no vignette?! I can't find it anywhere if there is one and it is quite annoying seeing as I want to go full speed but can't see out the sides

developer_response.body:
UPDATE: A patch that includes the option to toggle vignette on/off is now live (1.0.680) - please update to the latest version and let us know via support@beyondbluegame.com if you encounter any issues.
--
We hear you! This is something we are planning to include in the next patch so stay tuned. Thanks for the support!

reviews.review_description:
I’m terrified of the deep.
It took a lot of courage on my end to use and swim in Ocean Rift. I can’t really do that again as I was killed by a great white and a megalodon and a prehistoric dinosaur. So I was scared of playing this game. 

Turns out it’s very mellow and visible (that’s key) unlike more realistic ocean rift’s murky depths.

I love that this game is educational and involved. There’s 2 beautiful segments that deal with awareness and that’s great.

My only gripe is that open areas that you’re not supposed to go through are open and easily seen. I wish every point forward was made hidden or only shown when it’s time to go.‘otherwise it breaks immersion. 

I would would also  love it if my hands didn’t go through the sea life and the rocks. Again, breaks immersion.

The vignette off option is coming next month.  So my gripe with that will be over too.

Great game!

developer_response.body:
UPDATE: A patch that includes the option to toggle vignette on/off is now live (1.0.680) - please update to the latest version and let us know via support@beyondbluegame.com if you encounter any issues.
--
Thanks for your feedback! We hear you and are planning to include this feature in the next patch.

reviews.review_description: Playing the game through the quest game optimizer, increased the resolution, I think it is necessary, because the picture is not clear. The game is beautiful. I enjoyed it for 2 hours of playthrough. Please add more content and language translations. In general, the developers are great!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the feedback! I've shared it with the development team and can let you know that we are considering the possibility of localization for an update in the future, so stay tuned.

reviews.review_description: First off....as a scuba diver...this game makes my diver happy.   I have felt no need to rush through the underwater areas, as I enjoy the scenery when I dive.   So far I have found a few glitches that I am sure will be fixed, but all in all....Nicely done!!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the kind words! If you would like to email us at support@beyondbluegame.com with details about the bugs you encountered, we'd be happy to share with the development team to take a look.

reviews.review_description:
This game is a lot of fun and the best underwater game on the Meta quest. Nowhere near as good as the subside on steam VR.
But for what it is great game lots of fun 
My only real complaint about it is the game is just way too short

reviews.review_title: Bardzo fajna gra moja jedna z ulubionych

developer_response.body: Dziękuję bardzo!

reviews.review_description: A cute game with a nice short story (no fillers) . Really like it.

developer_response.body: Thank you very much!

reviews.review_description: I just love this game. Wow!
